paper_title: Isolation and Characterization of a New Diatom Species from Oman

methods.description: Procedure for collecting seawater samples from specific sites in Oman and establishing unialgal cultures of diatoms under controlled laboratory conditions.

steps.description: Seawater samples were collected from different locations between January and February 2015 using sterile 6 l bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect environmental samples (seawater) from specified locations.

steps.description: Salinity, pH and dissolved oxygen were measured in situ with a multiparameter handheld meter (Horiba, U52, Mfr # 3200164501).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure environmental parameters (salinity, pH, dissolved oxygen) at sampling sites.

steps.description: Immediately after sampling, the seawater bottles were transported to the laboratory in Muscat and kept at room temperature during the transportation period of around 6 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Transport collected samples to the laboratory under specified conditions.

steps.description: Upon return to the laboratory, the samples were partially decanted, and unialgal cultures were prepared by isolation and purification techniques using serial dilution.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare unialgal cultures from samples using decanting, isolation, purification, and serial dilution.

steps.description: Cultures were grown in f/20 medium (a ten-fold dilution of f/2 medium) (Guillard 1975) at 24 °C, with an irradiance of ~200 µmol.m -2 .s -1 and 18:6 h light: dark cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: Grow microalgal cultures under controlled laboratory conditions.

steps.description: Afterwards, they were transferred to Szczecin Culture Collection (SZCZ) where they were maintained in f/2 culture medium.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer and maintain cultures in a culture collection.

methods.description: Methods for observing live diatom cells and preparing permanent slides for detailed morphological examination using Light Microscopy (LM) and Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM).

steps.description: cells was observed using Nikon TS300 inverted microscope (Nikon Corporation, Tokyo, Japan) equipped with an ×100 Plan Apochromatic oil immersion objective (n.a.=1.40) and differential interference contrast (DIC) optics.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe live diatom cells using inverted light microscopy with specific optics.

steps.description: For LM of the valves, permanent slides were prepared from rinsed and cleaned material.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare permanent slides for light microscopy examination of diatom valves.

steps.description: Accordingly, a cell culture suspension was boiled with 30% hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) for a few hours at 150°C and then rinsed 5 times with deionized water.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean diatom material by boiling with hydrogen peroxide and rinsing with deionized water.

steps.description: The resulting cleaned diatom material was pipetted onto coverslips, dried and mounted on glass slides using Naphrax® mounting medium (Brunel Microscopes Ltd, Wiltshire, U.K.).
steps.searchable_summary: Mount cleaned diatom material onto glass slides using a mounting medium for LM.

steps.description: LM observations of the cleaned material were made with a Zeiss Axio Imager M2 (Carl Zeiss, Jena, Germany) using a ×100 Plan Apochromatic oil immersion objective (n.a. =1.46) equipped with DIC.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform light microscopy observations of cleaned diatom material.

steps.description: For SEM examination, a few drops of cleaned material were put onto Whatman Nuclepore polycarbonate membrane filters (Fisher Scientific, Schwerte, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare cleaned diatom material for SEM by filtering onto membrane filters.

steps.description: Once dried, the membranes were mounted onto aluminum stubs and coated with gold-palladium or gold for ca. 3-5 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount dried membranes onto SEM stubs and coat with conductive material.
inputs: Dried membrane filters with diatom material

steps.description: SEM observations were performed at the University of Rzeszów, Faculty of Biology and Agriculture using a Hitachi SEM SU8010 Equipment.

methods.description: Procedure for constructing a phylogenetic tree to determine the evolutionary relationships of the new diatom taxon relative to other diatoms, particularly within the Bacillariaceae family.

steps.description: In the present study, the phylogenetic tree was constructed with 263 diatoms taxa of which 72 were exclusively within the Bacillariaceae family.
steps.searchable_summary: Construct a phylogenetic tree using sequence data from a specified set of diatom taxa.
inputs: Genetic sequence data (e.g., DNA/RNA) for 263 diatom taxa (including 72 Bacillariaceae)

steps.description: Eventually, the genera used for the study involved Nitzschia, Pseudonitzschia, Fragilariopsis, Denticula, Psammodictyon, Cylindrotheca, Tryblionella, Hantzschia and Bacillaria.
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific diatom genera for inclusion in the phylogenetic analysis.
inputs: List of target genera (Nitzschia, Pseudonitzschia, Fragilariopsis, Denticula, Psammodictyon, Cylindrotheca, Tryblionella, Hantzschia, Bacillaria)
outputs: Subset of sequence data for target genera

steps.description: Our phylogenetic analysis showed that the new taxon was positioned in a sister clade to N. filiformis with low support value (bv= 43, not shown in the branched node, Figs. 14, S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the phylogenetic position of the new taxon within the constructed tree.
inputs: Sequence data for the new taxon
outputs: Phylogenetic position of the new taxon

steps.description: This low bootstrap value could be attributed to the missing mole...
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate statistical support for tree branches using bootstrap values.
